content:
4 min read
1 hour ago

--

Hello, my name is Rudro and I am a student going into my 4th year of engineering majoring in data science. I play tennis, love watching a few sports like cricket, formula 1, football and tennis obviously. As much as i hate to admit i also like pickleball a lot. Anyway 3 years in i wrote a lot of lines of code, learnt a ton of theory, data structures, machine learning models, training, testing etc etc. It feels great when it works i can tell you that.
But something else i got a knack for though and that was good design. Since 12th standard i wanted to build something that looked good, had utmost clarity, a product from start to finish that solves a problem. I started learning to build websites and got pretty decent at it. Wanted perfection with attention to detail and had my first go at a product making a ui library called flosynq. It got a couple hundred visitors, still gets a few every now and then. But something i realised is perfection didnt come in one go no matter how hard i tried. The sites became better with time and understanding of how people use it. I built a couple of sites and templates for a little cash through these 3 years.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

brandioz feature section6 months ago though, I saw a trend, something i could build for, ads ran on TV and youtube and not too many people got into it. Open AI and Gemini started to advertise themselves as search platforms for any kind of query. Google started to promote Gemini in the IPL heavily. It was a new categoy called AI SEO or as the technical term states Generative Engine Optimisation(GEO). A way in which a lot of young startups and products could come up if they were clear, and optimised for GEO instead of ranking amongst giants on google search. I read about it and saw the available options and it heavily favoured having dashboards that showed you how many times you got cited by different ai instead of fixing the technical architecture.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Brandioz dashboardSites that missed key files, structure, massive client rendering, no crawler specific pages, sitemaps that were wrong with dead links. I read more into it built up a prototype. Understood how the system worked. I built up a full pipeline to detect and understand on how sites could optimise for ai. A lot of stuff mattered like the heading to description ratios, words in each section, faq’s , schemas etc. Apart from all of this i created a AIO profile builder which would basically build you a crawler page allowing AI to crawl and understand what you do easier. A complete detailed audit showing you where you could improve and a site model to show if sites are missing any links or have any dead links in files like llms.txt. Also allows you to generate a custom built about page containing faqs and a CTA both in typescript and html along with a metadata and a JSON-ld shema. People miss out and have contradictory claims in seperate places, categories go undefined or are simply wrong with the language they use. This helps you solve that. You could also get a backlink from brandioz to share the report if you wanted to.

I have had a start with this and want to continue building this. Over 3000 people have visited the site and i have got positive feedback. Its come a long way since i released it 3 months ago. I also focused a great deal on the design. Not to be deceptive or anything but a neat , good looking funky site might just make it. I am going to be releasing a pro version which will be much more aggressive in a few weeks .And as Patrick Jane said “I don’t have a plan. I just have a general vibe of what I want to happen.”
If you want to visit the site heres a link to it — https://brandioz.com
